input.question:
We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Please list all rules relevant to `Brake Over Travel Switch/BOTS`. Answer with only the rule numbers (i.e.: AA.1.1.1) separated by commas and no other words.



The rules relevant to `Brake Over Travel Switch/BOTS` are:

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.3.3.1
            TITLE: the vehicle must have a brake over travel switch (bots).  brake pedal travel exceeding the

            CONTENT:
            rule T.3.3.1: The vehicle must have a Brake Over Travel Switch (BOTS).  Brake pedal travel exceeding the
T.3.3.1 The vehicle must have a Brake Over Travel Switch (BOTS).  Brake pedal travel exceeding the 
normal range will actuate the switch

RULE: ev.7.2.2
            TITLE: when the shutdown circuit opens:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.2.2: When the Shutdown Circuit Opens:
EV.7.2.2 When the Shutdown Circuit Opens: 
a. The Tractive System must Shutdown 
b. All Accumulator current flow must stop immediately  EV.5.4.3

c. The voltage in the Tractive System must be Low Voltage T.9.1.2 in five seconds or less 
d. The Motor(s) must spin free.  Torque must not be applied to the Motor(s)

RULE: ev.7.1.1
            TITLE: the shutdown circuit consists of the following components, connected in series:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.1.1: The Shutdown Circuit consists of the following components, connected in series:
EV.7.1.1 The Shutdown Circuit consists of the following components, connected in series: 
a. Accumulator Management System (AMS)  EV.7.3 
b. Insulation Monitoring Device (IMD)  EV.7.6
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 101 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
c. Brake System Plausibility Device (BSPD)  EV.7.7 
d. Interlocks (as required)  EV.7.8 
e. Master Switches (GLVMS, TSMS)  EV.7.9 
f. Shutdown Buttons  EV.7.10 
g. Brake Over Travel Switch (BOTS)  T.3.3 
h. Inertia Switch  T.9.4

RULE: ic.9.1.1
            TITLE: the shutdown circuit consists of the following components:

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.9.1.1: The Shutdown Circuit consists of the following components:
IC.9.1.1 The Shutdown Circuit consists of the following components:  
a. Primary Master Switch  IC.9.3 
b. Cockpit Main Switch  IC.9.4 
c. (ETC Only) Brake System Plausibility Device (BSPD)  IC.4.8 
d. Brake Overtravel Switch (BOTS)  T.3.3 
e. Inertia Switch (if used)  T.9.4

RULE: t.3.2.4
            TITLE: (ev only) additional requirements for electric vehicles:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.3.2.4: (EV only) Additional requirements for Electric Vehicles:
T.3.2.4 (EV only) Additional requirements for Electric Vehicles: 
a. The first 90% of the Brake Pedal travel may be used to regenerate energy without 
actuating the hydraulic brake system. 
b. The remaining Brake Pedal travel must directly actuate the hydraulic brake system.  
Brake energy regeneration may stay active.

RULE: ev.4.6
            TITLE: brake system encoder - bse

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.4.6: Brake System Encoder - BSE
EV.4.6 Brake System Encoder - BSE 
Refer to T.4.3 for specific requirements of the BSE

RULE: ic.4.6
            TITLE: brake system encoder - bse

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.4.6: Brake System Encoder - BSE
IC.4.6 Brake System Encoder - BSE 
Refer to T.4.3 for specific requirements of the BSE

RULE: t.4.2.3
            TITLE: the apps sensors must have different transfer functions which meet one of the two:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.4.2.3: The APPS sensors must have different transfer functions which meet one of the two:
T.4.2.3 The APPS sensors must have different transfer functions which meet one of the two: 
• Each sensor has different gradients and/or offsets to the other(s).  The circuit must have 
a pull-up or pull-down resistor to bring an open circuit input to 0% Pedal Travel 
• An OEM pedal sensor with opposite slopes.  Non OEM opposite slope sensor 
configurations require prior approval.   
The intent is that in a short circuit the APPSs will only agree at 0% Pedal Travel

RULE: t.3.2.3
            TITLE: failure of non-loadbearing components in the brake system or pedal box must not interfere

            CONTENT:
            rule T.3.2.3: Failure of non-loadbearing components in the Brake System or pedal box must not interfere
T.3.2.3 Failure of non-loadbearing components in the Brake System or pedal box must not interfere 
with Brake Pedal operation or Brake System function

RULE: t.3.1.2
            TITLE: the brake system must:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.3.1.2: The Brake System must:
T.3.1.2 The Brake System must:  
a. Act on all four wheels  
b. Be operated by a single control 
c. Be capable of locking all four wheels

RULE: in.10.7
            TITLE: switch function

            CONTENT:
            rule IN.10.7: Switch Function
IN.10.7 Switch Function 
The function of one or more of the Primary Master Switch IC.9.3, Cockpit Main Switch IC.9.4, 
and/or BOTS T.3.3 will be verified during the Noise Test

RULE: t.3.1.8
            TITLE: the brake system must be protected with scatter shields from failure of the drive train (see

            CONTENT:
            rule T.3.1.8: The Brake System must be protected with scatter shields from failure of the drive train (see
T.3.1.8 The Brake System must be protected with scatter shields from failure of the drive train (see 
T.5.2) or from minor collisions.

RULE: ev.7.7.1
            TITLE: the vehicle must have a standalone nonprogrammable circuit to check for simultaneous

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.7.1: The vehicle must have a standalone nonprogrammable circuit to check for simultaneous
EV.7.7.1 The vehicle must have a standalone nonprogrammable circuit to check for simultaneous 
braking and high power output 
The BSPD must be provided in addition to the APPS / Brake Pedal Plausibility Check (EV.4.7)

RULE: t.3.3.3
            TITLE: operation of the bots to the off position must open the shutdown circuit ic.9.2.2 / ev.7.2.2

            CONTENT:
            rule T.3.3.3: Operation of the BOTS to the OFF position must Open the Shutdown Circuit IC.9.2.2 / EV.7.2.2
T.3.3.3 Operation of the BOTS to the OFF position must Open the Shutdown Circuit IC.9.2.2 / EV.7.2.2

RULE: ev.7.9.3
            TITLE: the tractive system master switch (tsms) must:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.9.3: The Tractive System Master Switch (TSMS) must:
EV.7.9.3 The Tractive System Master Switch (TSMS) must:  
a. Open the Shutdown Circuit in the OFF position  EV.7.2.2  
b. Be the last switch before the AIRs except for Precharge circuitry and Interlocks.   
c. Be in the center of a completely orange circular area of > 50 mm in diameter 
d. Be labeled “TS” and the symbol specified in ISO 7010-W012  (triangle with black 
lightning bolt on yellow background). 
e. Be fitted with a "lockout/tagout" capability in the OFF position  EV.11.3.1

RULE: t.3.3.2
            TITLE: the bots must be a mechanical single pole, single throw (commonly known as a two position)

            CONTENT:
            rule T.3.3.2: The BOTS must be a mechanical single pole, single throw (commonly known as a two position)
T.3.3.2 The BOTS must be a mechanical single pole, single throw (commonly known as a two position) 
switch (push-pull or flip type).
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 64 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: t.4.3.3
            TITLE: the bse or switch signals must be sent directly to a controller using an analogue signal or via a

            CONTENT:
            rule T.4.3.3: The BSE or switch signals must be sent directly to a controller using an analogue signal or via a
T.4.3.3 The BSE or switch signals must be sent directly to a controller using an analogue signal or via a 
digital data transmission bus such as CAN or FlexRay 
Any failure of the BSE or BSE wiring that persists more than 100 msec must be detectable by 
the controller and treated like an implausibility and power to the (IC) electronic throttle / (EV) 
Motor(s) must be immediately stopped completely.   
(EV only) It is not necessary to completely deactivate the Tractive System, the motor 
controller(s) stopping power to the motor(s) is sufficient.
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 66 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: t.3.1.10
            TITLE: fasteners in the brake system are critical fasteners, see t.8.2

            CONTENT:
            rule T.3.1.10: Fasteners in the Brake System are Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2
T.3.1.10 Fasteners in the Brake System are Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2

RULE: ev.9.4.2
            TITLE: ready to drive must not be possible until the three at the same time:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.9.4.2: Ready to Drive must not be possible until the three at the same time:
EV.9.4.2 Ready to Drive must not be possible until the three at the same time: 
• Tractive System Active  EV.9.3 
• The Brake Pedal is pressed and held to engage the mechanical brakes  T.3.2 
• The driver performs a manual action to initiate Ready to Drive  
Such as pressing a specific button in the cockpit

RULE: t.3.3.5
            TITLE: the driver must not be able to reset the bots.

            CONTENT:
            rule T.3.3.5: The driver must not be able to reset the BOTS.
T.3.3.5 The driver must not be able to reset the BOTS.
